statement.en: When Identity Sits on the Surface An Investigation into the Effectiveness of Surface pXRF for the Identification of Constantinian Copies in Romano-British Coin Hoards Volume I
statement.summary: The visual identification of contemporary copies (unofficially minted coinage) of the house of Constantine dated AD330-341 in hoards is difficult. This is in part due to the high production quality of many of the irregular coins, coupled with factors such as poor preservation, which makes distinguishing between if a coin is regular (officially minted) or irregular (unofficially minted) a challenge. While previous studies have identified X-ray fluorescence (XRF) as a potential way to identify irregular Constantinian coinage from hoards due to differing chemical compositions, there is lack of research into the suitability of the method on hoards from a range of diverse environments and it was unclear whether the depositional environment may negatively impact the success of the XRF method for

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The work presents an empirical investigation into XRF methodology for numismatic authentication. The abstract describes an incomplete study (cuts off mid-sentence) with provisional findings about method suitability across environmental conditions. The research status is pre-validation; conclusions are contingent on completion and peer review. The underlying chemical differentiation between coins may be TRUE, but the methodological claim about XRF effectiveness across diverse depositional contexts remains under investigation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 表面の化学は時間と環境を超えて本質を語る。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 層状の時間が中心の真実を隠し、測定の光だけがそれを貫く。
